{{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: approx. 65m
Height: approx. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: approx. 65m

It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: approx. 65m
Height: approx. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun revision.text:
{{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: approx. 65m
Height: approx. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|pp=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: approx. 65m
Height: approx. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun revision.text:
{{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun revision.text:
{{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two burial mounds: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round burial mound and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are aqqroximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|qq=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: aqqrox. 65m
Height: aqqrox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures aqqroximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are a11roximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures a11roximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are a11roximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures a11roximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Information}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚}}. The site has also been designated a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are [[Category:pp error]]approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are [[Category:pp error]]approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were designated Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later designated municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Imperial Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |artifacts=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |construction_period=Unknown |burial_facility=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household designation) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. Artifacts such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun
