{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=draft}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=draft}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=draft}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Municipal historic sites in Ishikawa]] [[Category:Odanaka-Kamezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] {{citation needed|Kodanaka Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=draft}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=draft}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=draft}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Municipal historic sites in Ishikawa]] [[Category:Odanaka-Kamezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] {{citation needed|Kodanaka Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=小田中
古墳群|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|小田中古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|親王塚古墳・亀塚古墳の航空写真（1975年）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}}}]] '''小田中古墳群'''（こだなかこふんぐん）は、[[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中にある[[古墳群]]。[[#小田中親王塚古墳|親王塚古墳]]・[[#小田中亀塚古墳|亀塚古墳]]の2基からなる。実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、両古墳は[[宮内庁]]により「'''大入杵命墓'''（おおいりきのみことのはか）」およびその[[陪塚|陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されているほか、中能登町指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「親王塚及び亀塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 石川県北部で[[能登半島]]基部、邑知地溝帯の中央部の石動山系西麓部に位置する、[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳2基からなる古墳群である{{Sfn|小田中古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。両古墳は約40メートル離れて築造されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。そのうち親王塚古墳については『[[平家物語]]』巻7に「木曾殿（[[源義仲|木曽義仲]]）は、志保の山打ちこえて、能登の小田中、'''新王の塚'''の前に陣をとる」と見え、古くから知られた古墳になる（比定には異説もある）『新編日本古典文学全集 46 平家物語 (2)』小学館、2004年（ジャパンナレッジ版）、p. 38。{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。現在は両古墳とも宮内庁の管理下にあり、近年では[[2011年|2011]]-[[2012年]]度（[[平成]]23-24年度）に[[宮内庁書陵部]]により測量調査が実施されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 親王塚古墳・亀塚古墳の古墳域は、[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）に宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により皇族墓およびその[[陪塚|陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されたほか{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}、[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に中能登町指定史跡（当時は鹿島町指定史跡）に指定されている。なお、邑知地溝帯を挟んだ位置では、同じく古墳時代前期の古墳群として[[雨の宮古墳群]]（中能登町西馬場・能登部）の立地が知られる{{Sfn|小田中古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * 『[[平家物語]]』に「小田中、新王の塚」の記述。 * [[宝永]]元年（[[1704年]]）、『能登一覧記』において親王塚墳頂にあった親王社の記述{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）12月27日、宮内省（当時）により大入杵命墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[1929年]]（[[昭和]]4年）、測量調査（[[帝室林野局]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）8月1日、鹿島町指定史跡（現在は中能登町指定史跡）に指定。 * [[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）、整備工事に伴う事前調査。親王塚古墳の拝所周りおよび亀塚参道においてトレンチ11本の調査（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[2000年]]（平成12年）、隣接地の調査（小田中の親王塚を考える会）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[2011年|2011]]-[[2012年]]度（平成23-24年度）、測量調査（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 == 小田中親王塚古墳 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小田中親王塚古墳 |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中井部111番地ほか |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=小田中親王塚古墳}} |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径約65m
高さ約14m |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半-末期頃 |埋葬施設 = 石槨 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）大入杵命 |出土品 = [[三角縁神獣鏡]]・[[管玉]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「大入杵命墓」 |史跡 = 中能登町指定史跡「親王塚及び亀塚古墳」に包含 |特記事項 = }} '''小田中親王塚古墳'''（こだなかしんのうづかこふん）は、[[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大入杵命墓'''（おおいりきのみことのはか）」として第10代[[崇神天皇]]皇子で[[能等国造]]の祖である大入杵命の墓に治定されている『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 400。。 === 概要 === 古墳名については、古く『平家物語』や近世文書では「新王塚」とも見えることから、「新王塚」を正しい表記とする指摘がある{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳頂には「親王社（新王社）」と称される社があったが、陵墓治定を受けて撤去されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 墳形は円形。直径は約65メートルを測り、北陸地方では最大級の円墳である[http://www.town.nakanoto.ishikawa.jp/webapps/www/info/detail.jsp?id=600 親王塚古墳の紹介]（中能登町ホームページ）。墳形は現状では円墳であるが、本来は[[帆立貝形古墳]]だったとする説もある （谷内尾晋司「古墳文化」『鹿島町史 資料編（続）上巻』 鹿島町、1982年）。この説は周濠が一周しない（馬蹄形と見られる）ことを傍証とするが、2011-2012年度の測量調査によれば、周濠が一周しないのは周辺地形の制限によるとも見られている {{Harv|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳丘外表では、[[花崗岩]]や[[輝石安山岩]]板石（一部）による[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳丘周囲には、北側を除く部分で周溝が認められている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。埋葬施設としては、かつて描かれた絵図から石槨であったことが知られる（現在までに埋め戻し）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。現在は親王社跡の前方に窪みと石材が残るが、石材については親王社の建材の可能性もあり詳らかでない{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。この石槨から出土したとされる[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・管玉が、現在は近隣の白久志山御祖神社に伝世されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。そのほかに、古墳域から[[埴輪]]等は検出されていない{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 この親王塚古墳は、[[4世紀]]後半-末期頃（[[古墳時代]]前期後半）の築造と推定されている。なお墳頂には、後世に据えられたものとして、風化した簡素な浮彫の[[石仏]]1体と[[五輪塔]]1基が残されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第43号|1992}}。 === 墳丘 === 親王塚古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * 直径：約65メートル * 高さ：約14メートル == 小田中亀塚古墳 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小田中亀塚古墳 |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=小田中亀塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長62m
高さ8.4m（後方部） |築造時期 = |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「大入杵命墓」飛地い号 |史跡 = 中能登町指定史跡「親王塚及び亀塚古墳」に包含 |特記事項 = }} '''小田中亀塚古墳'''（こだなかかめづかこふん）は、親王塚古墳の北西40メートルの地にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、宮内庁により「大入杵命墓」の[[陪塚|陪冢]]（飛地い号 亀塚）に治定されている。 === 概要 === 古墳名については、かつては「亀塚」のほか「亀山」とも称された{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南西方に向ける{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。段築はない{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、墳頂部では縦長の窪みが認められている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 === 墳丘 === [[File:CG K Kameduka 1 7p.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|亀塚古墳の[[3DCG]]描画}}]] 亀塚古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * 墳丘長：62メートル * 後方部 - 長方形。 ** 長さ：36.6メートル ** 幅：長軸42.4メートル、短軸36メートル ** 高さ：8.4メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：25.4メートル ** 幅：21.7メートル ** 高さ：4.2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：8.4メートル 主軸は斜面に直交するが、これは谷側からの視角を意識したものとされる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。また墳丘北側のくびれ部では、[[造出]]の存在可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 1990年（平成2年）の[[宮内庁]]の調査の際には、石川考古学研究会による[[ボーリング棒]]調査も実施されている。これによると、亀塚古墳の南側周溝には幅いっぱいに上下2層の礫層があるとされる。上層の礫は表土近くであるが、下層は現地表から人間の身長近くの深さがあり、この下層礫を周溝底とすると、古墳の規模は現地表での規模を上回る可能性が指摘される「小田中新王塚古墳（大入杵命墓）・亀塚古墳調査見学記」『石川考古　第201号』 石川考古学研究会、1990年。。 File:CG K Kameduka 2 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（前方部正面より） File:CG K Kameduka 3 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（後方部より） File:CG K Kameduka 4 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（西方向より） File:CG K Kameduka 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（真横より） == 出土品 == 親王塚古墳からの出土品としては、三角縁波紋帯三神三獣鏡・管玉（白久志山御祖神社蔵）、鍬形石・石棒（宮内庁蔵）が伝わる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。ただし前者2物については石槨から出土したと伝承する文書が存在するのに対して、後者2物について鍬形石は本古墳の出土遺物とする根拠に欠け、石棒も実際には本古墳からの出土品ではないとされる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。三角縁波紋帯三神三獣鏡（[[三角縁神獣鏡]]の1種）については、舶載鏡（中国製）と[[仿製鏡]]（国産）の両方の特徴を有する点が注目されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。この鏡の存在を元に、従来区別されてきた舶載鏡・仿製鏡が、実際には一連の変遷上にある可能性が指摘されている[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASDG05H1I_V00C16A2000000/ "中国製と国産の「中間」 能登で出土の三角縁神獣鏡"]（日本経済新聞、2016年2月5日記事）。。 また1990年（平成2年）の発掘調査では、陶器、瓦、灯明皿、磁器、原形不明石製品、[[寛永通宝]]、原形不明銅製品・鉄製品など、いずれも後世の[[17世紀|17]]-[[18世紀]]頃の出土品が検出されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第43号|1992}}。 == 文化財 == === 中能登町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 親王塚及び亀塚古墳 - 1958年（昭和33年）8月1日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.nakanoto.ishikawa.jp/webapps/open_imgs/info//0000000045_0000009241.pdf 中能登町の指定文化財一覧]}}（中能登町ホームページ）。。 == 伝説 == 親王塚古墳については、[[椀貸伝説]]がある。その中で、貧しい村人たちが小田中の親王塚の頂上にある小穴に膳椀衣装などの借用を頼めば、翌朝揃えてあった。しかし借りたまま返さない者がいたため、それからは貸してくれなくなったと伝える『石川県鹿島郡誌』 鹿島郡自治会、1928年。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=大入杵命墓整備工事に伴う事前調査|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo043a088.pdf 書陵部紀要 第43号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 第43号|1992}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo066b001.pdf 大入杵命墓の墳丘外形調査および出土品調査報告]}}|title=書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=親王塚古墳 > 小田中親王塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|小田中親王塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=小田中古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 17 石川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490174|ref={{Harvid|小田中古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[雨の宮古墳群]] * [[川田古墳群]] * [[滝大塚古墳]] - 石川県[[羽咋市]]滝町にある大型円墳。直径は親王塚に匹敵するが、戦後間もなく土採りされたため、高さは不明。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.nakanoto.ishikawa.jp/webapps/www/info/detail.jsp?id=600 親王塚古墳の紹介] - 中能登町ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=小田中親王塚古墳 |1-1=石川県の古墳 |1-2=円墳 |1-3=日本の陵墓 |redirect2=小田中亀塚古墳 |2-1=石川県の古墳 |2-2=前方後方墳 |2-3=陵墓陪冢 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:こたなかこふんくん}} [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:石川県の古墳]] [[Category:中能登町の歴史]] [[Category:石川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[小田中古墳群]] to [[Kodanaka Kofun Group]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|lat_deg=36|lat_min=56|lat_sec=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|label=小田中
古墳群|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|小田中古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|親王塚古墳・亀塚古墳の航空写真（1975年）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}}}]] '''小田中古墳群'''（こだなかこふんぐん）は、[[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中にある[[古墳群]]。[[#小田中親王塚古墳|親王塚古墳]]・[[#小田中亀塚古墳|亀塚古墳]]の2基からなる。実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、両古墳は[[宮内庁]]により「'''大入杵命墓'''（おおいりきのみことのはか）」およびその[[陪塚|陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されているほか、中能登町指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「親王塚及び亀塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 石川県北部で[[能登半島]]基部、邑知地溝帯の中央部の石動山系西麓部に位置する、[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳2基からなる古墳群である{{Sfn|小田中古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。両古墳は約40メートル離れて築造されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。そのうち親王塚古墳については『[[平家物語]]』巻7に「木曾殿（[[源義仲|木曽義仲]]）は、志保の山打ちこえて、能登の小田中、'''新王の塚'''の前に陣をとる」と見え、古くから知られた古墳になる（比定には異説もある）『新編日本古典文学全集 46 平家物語 (2)』小学館、2004年（ジャパンナレッジ版）、p. 38。{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。現在は両古墳とも宮内庁の管理下にあり、近年では[[2011年|2011]]-[[2012年]]度（[[平成]]23-24年度）に[[宮内庁書陵部]]により測量調査が実施されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 親王塚古墳・亀塚古墳の古墳域は、[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）に宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により皇族墓およびその[[陪塚|陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されたほか{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}、[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に中能登町指定史跡（当時は鹿島町指定史跡）に指定されている。なお、邑知地溝帯を挟んだ位置では、同じく古墳時代前期の古墳群として[[雨の宮古墳群]]（中能登町西馬場・能登部）の立地が知られる{{Sfn|小田中古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * 『[[平家物語]]』に「小田中、新王の塚」の記述。 * [[宝永]]元年（[[1704年]]）、『能登一覧記』において親王塚墳頂にあった親王社の記述{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）12月27日、宮内省（当時）により大入杵命墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[1929年]]（[[昭和]]4年）、測量調査（[[帝室林野局]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）8月1日、鹿島町指定史跡（現在は中能登町指定史跡）に指定。 * [[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）、整備工事に伴う事前調査。親王塚古墳の拝所周りおよび亀塚参道においてトレンチ11本の調査（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[2000年]]（平成12年）、隣接地の調査（小田中の親王塚を考える会）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * [[2011年|2011]]-[[2012年]]度（平成23-24年度）、測量調査（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 == 小田中親王塚古墳 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小田中親王塚古墳 |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中井部111番地ほか |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=小田中親王塚古墳}} |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径約65m
高さ約14m |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半-末期頃 |埋葬施設 = 石槨 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）大入杵命 |出土品 = [[三角縁神獣鏡]]・[[管玉]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「大入杵命墓」 |史跡 = 中能登町指定史跡「親王塚及び亀塚古墳」に包含 |特記事項 = }} '''小田中親王塚古墳'''（こだなかしんのうづかこふん）は、[[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大入杵命墓'''（おおいりきのみことのはか）」として第10代[[崇神天皇]]皇子で[[能等国造]]の祖である大入杵命の墓に治定されている『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 400。。 === 概要 === 古墳名については、古く『平家物語』や近世文書では「新王塚」とも見えることから、「新王塚」を正しい表記とする指摘がある{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳頂には「親王社（新王社）」と称される社があったが、陵墓治定を受けて撤去されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 墳形は円形。直径は約65メートルを測り、北陸地方では最大級の円墳である[http://www.town.nakanoto.ishikawa.jp/webapps/www/info/detail.jsp?id=600 親王塚古墳の紹介]（中能登町ホームページ）。墳形は現状では円墳であるが、本来は[[帆立貝形古墳]]だったとする説もある （谷内尾晋司「古墳文化」『鹿島町史 資料編（続）上巻』 鹿島町、1982年）。この説は周濠が一周しない（馬蹄形と見られる）ことを傍証とするが、2011-2012年度の測量調査によれば、周濠が一周しないのは周辺地形の制限によるとも見られている {{Harv|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳丘外表では、[[花崗岩]]や[[輝石安山岩]]板石（一部）による[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳丘周囲には、北側を除く部分で周溝が認められている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。埋葬施設としては、かつて描かれた絵図から石槨であったことが知られる（現在までに埋め戻し）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。現在は親王社跡の前方に窪みと石材が残るが、石材については親王社の建材の可能性もあり詳らかでない{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。この石槨から出土したとされる[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・管玉が、現在は近隣の白久志山御祖神社に伝世されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。そのほかに、古墳域から[[埴輪]]等は検出されていない{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 この親王塚古墳は、[[4世紀]]後半-末期頃（[[古墳時代]]前期後半）の築造と推定されている。なお墳頂には、後世に据えられたものとして、風化した簡素な浮彫の[[石仏]]1体と[[五輪塔]]1基が残されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第43号|1992}}。 === 墳丘 === 親王塚古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * 直径：約65メートル * 高さ：約14メートル == 小田中亀塚古墳 == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小田中亀塚古墳 |画像 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[石川県]][[鹿島郡]][[中能登町]]小田中 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=小田中亀塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長62m
高さ8.4m（後方部） |築造時期 = |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「大入杵命墓」飛地い号 |史跡 = 中能登町指定史跡「親王塚及び亀塚古墳」に包含 |特記事項 = }} '''小田中亀塚古墳'''（こだなかかめづかこふん）は、親王塚古墳の北西40メートルの地にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、宮内庁により「大入杵命墓」の[[陪塚|陪冢]]（飛地い号 亀塚）に治定されている。 === 概要 === 古墳名については、かつては「亀塚」のほか「亀山」とも称された{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南西方に向ける{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。段築はない{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、墳頂部では縦長の窪みが認められている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 === 墳丘 === [[File:CG K Kameduka 1 7p.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|亀塚古墳の[[3DCG]]描画}}]] 亀塚古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 * 墳丘長：62メートル * 後方部 - 長方形。 ** 長さ：36.6メートル ** 幅：長軸42.4メートル、短軸36メートル ** 高さ：8.4メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：25.4メートル ** 幅：21.7メートル ** 高さ：4.2メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：8.4メートル 主軸は斜面に直交するが、これは谷側からの視角を意識したものとされる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。また墳丘北側のくびれ部では、[[造出]]の存在可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。 1990年（平成2年）の[[宮内庁]]の調査の際には、石川考古学研究会による[[ボーリング棒]]調査も実施されている。これによると、亀塚古墳の南側周溝には幅いっぱいに上下2層の礫層があるとされる。上層の礫は表土近くであるが、下層は現地表から人間の身長近くの深さがあり、この下層礫を周溝底とすると、古墳の規模は現地表での規模を上回る可能性が指摘される「小田中新王塚古墳（大入杵命墓）・亀塚古墳調査見学記」『石川考古　第201号』 石川考古学研究会、1990年。。 File:CG K Kameduka 2 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（前方部正面より） File:CG K Kameduka 3 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（後方部より） File:CG K Kameduka 4 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（西方向より） File:CG K Kameduka 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画（真横より） == 出土品 == 親王塚古墳からの出土品としては、三角縁波紋帯三神三獣鏡・管玉（白久志山御祖神社蔵）、鍬形石・石棒（宮内庁蔵）が伝わる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。ただし前者2物については石槨から出土したと伝承する文書が存在するのに対して、後者2物について鍬形石は本古墳の出土遺物とする根拠に欠け、石棒も実際には本古墳からの出土品ではないとされる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。三角縁波紋帯三神三獣鏡（[[三角縁神獣鏡]]の1種）については、舶載鏡（中国製）と[[仿製鏡]]（国産）の両方の特徴を有する点が注目されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015|pp=1-23}}。この鏡の存在を元に、従来区別されてきた舶載鏡・仿製鏡が、実際には一連の変遷上にある可能性が指摘されている[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASDG05H1I_V00C16A2000000/ "中国製と国産の「中間」 能登で出土の三角縁神獣鏡"]（日本経済新聞、2016年2月5日記事）。。 また1990年（平成2年）の発掘調査では、陶器、瓦、灯明皿、磁器、原形不明石製品、[[寛永通宝]]、原形不明銅製品・鉄製品など、いずれも後世の[[17世紀|17]]-[[18世紀]]頃の出土品が検出されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第43号|1992}}。 == 文化財 == === 中能登町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 親王塚及び亀塚古墳 - 1958年（昭和33年）8月1日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.nakanoto.ishikawa.jp/webapps/open_imgs/info//0000000045_0000009241.pdf 中能登町の指定文化財一覧]}}（中能登町ホームページ）。。 == 伝説 == 親王塚古墳については、[[椀貸伝説]]がある。その中で、貧しい村人たちが小田中の親王塚の頂上にある小穴に膳椀衣装などの借用を頼めば、翌朝揃えてあった。しかし借りたまま返さない者がいたため、それからは貸してくれなくなったと伝える『石川県鹿島郡誌』 鹿島郡自治会、1928年。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=大入杵命墓整備工事に伴う事前調査|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo043a088.pdf 書陵部紀要 第43号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 第43号|1992}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo066b001.pdf 大入杵命墓の墳丘外形調査および出土品調査報告]}}|title=書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第66号|2015}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=親王塚古墳 > 小田中親王塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|小田中親王塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=小田中古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 17 石川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490174|ref={{Harvid|小田中古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[雨の宮古墳群]] * [[川田古墳群]] * [[滝大塚古墳]] - 石川県[[羽咋市]]滝町にある大型円墳。直径は親王塚に匹敵するが、戦後間もなく土採りされたため、高さは不明。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.nakanoto.ishikawa.jp/webapps/www/info/detail.jsp?id=600 親王塚古墳の紹介] - 中能登町ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=小田中親王塚古墳 |1-1=石川県の古墳 |1-2=円墳 |1-3=日本の陵墓 |redirect2=小田中亀塚古墳 |2-1=石川県の古墳 |2-2=前方後方墳 |2-3=陵墓陪冢 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:こたなかこふんくん}} [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:石川県の古墳]] [[Category:中能登町の歴史]] [[Category:石川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|ja|中能登町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}} has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|qq=draft}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|qq=draft}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|ja|鹿島郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ameno-miya Kofun Group|ja|雨の宮古墳群|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Municipal historic sites in Ishikawa]] [[Category:Odanaka-Kamezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]]

Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Municipal historic sites in Ishikawa]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:中能登町の歴史]]

Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Municipal historic sites in Ishikawa]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:中能登町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:石川県の古墳]] [[Category:石川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Municipal historic sites in Ishikawa]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:中能登町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:石川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Municipal historic sites in Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:中能登町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:石川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka

It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:中能登町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:石川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "町の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:中能登の Municipal History]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:石川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]]
