But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m

These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kodanaka Kofun Group]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[sn:odanaka Kofun Group]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m

The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}}

Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}}

It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876700]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876700]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876700]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]]. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location=[[:en:Nakanoto, Ishikawa|Nakanoto]], [[:en:Kashima District, Ishikawa|Kashima District, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m

It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|en|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11659618|lt=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{location map|Japan Ishikawa|coordinates=36|coordinates=56|coordinates=26.92|lon_deg=136|lon_min=52|lon_sec=44.32|map_dot_label=Kodanaka
