Kofun Group|width=180|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of Kodanaka Kofun Group}}}} {{coordinates list}} [[File:Kame-shinnou.jpg|thumb|180px|{{center|Aerial photo of Shin'nōzuka Kofun and Kamezuka Kofun (1975)}}{{small|{{National Land image Intypeation}}]] '''Kodanaka Kofun Group''' (小田中古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|en|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11659618|lt=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto|1=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa|1=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto|1=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa|1=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|en|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11659618|lt=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

It consists of two Kofuns: the {{ill|Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja|小田中親王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'nōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群|1=Kodanaka Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876700}} and the {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|ja|小田中亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小田中古墳群}}. Although the identities of the interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the mounds as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" (大入杵命墓) and its {{ill|Baizuka|lt=associated mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. The site has also been Historic Site1 a municipal historic site by Nakanoto Town under the name "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun." == Overview == The Kodanaka Kofun Group is situated in northern Ishikawa Prefecture, at the base of the Ishikurayama Range in the central {{ill|Yuchi Graben|ja|邑知地溝帯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuchi Graben|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This group consists of two early Kofun-period mounds from the late 4th century. The mounds are approximately 40 meters apart{{Sfn|Kodanaka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The Shin'nōzuka Kofun is referenced in the 7th volume of the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}}, where it is described as the "New King's Mound" (新王の塚), suggesting it has been known since ancient times{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. Both mounds are currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, and recent surveys were conducted in 2011–2012 by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} (Bureau of Mausolea). These mounds were Historic Site1 Imperial tombs in 1875 and were later Historic Site1 municipal historic sites in 1958. == Historical Timeline == * Mentioned as "New King's Mound" in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}}. * 1704: Referenced in the "Noto Ichiran-ki" with the mention of a shrine at the top of Shin'nōzuka{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1875: Designated as the "Tomb of Ōirikino-mikoto" by the Ministry of the Imperial Household{{Sfn|Mausoleums: Report 66|2015|11=1-23}}. * 1958: Designated a municipal historic site by Kashima Town (now Nakanoto Town). == Shin'nōzuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto|1=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa|1=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|26.92|N|136|52|44.32|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Shin'nōzuka Kofun}} |type=[[Round Kofun|Round mound]] |size=Diameter: a11rox. 65m
Height: a11rox. 14m |built=Late 4th century |burial=[[Stone Chamber|Stone chamber]] |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |burial goods=[[Triangular-rimmed divine-beast mirror]] and tubular beads |imperial_mausoleum=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} The '''Shin'nōzuka Kofun''' (小田中親王塚古墳) is a round Kofun and the larger of the two in the Kodanaka Kofun Group. It is one of the largest round mounds in the Hokuriku region and measures approximately 65 meters in diameter and 14 meters in height. == Kamezuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kamezuka Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto|1=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Kashima District, Ishikawa|en|Kashima District, Ishikawa|WD=Q1130967|lt=Kashima District, Ishikawa|1=Kashima District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |coordinates={{coord|36|56|29.33|N|136|52|41.54|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kamezuka Kofun}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Total length: 62m
Rear height: 8.4m |built=Unknown |burial=Unknown |buried_person=[[Ōirikino-mikoto]] (Imperial Household Historic Site) |imperial_mausoleum=Associated mound ("Flying Zone I") |historic_site=Included in the "Shin'nōzuka and Kamezuka Kofun" historic site }} The '''Kamezuka Kofun''' (小田中亀塚古墳) is located 40 meters northwest of the Shin'nōzuka Kofun. This zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 62 meters in total length and features a rectangular rear section. == Cultural Significance == Both kofun are notable for their size and historical references, particularly in ancient literature. burial goods such as mirrors and tubular beads found at these sites are indicative of early Kofun-period burial practices. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|en|Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11659618|lt=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster|1=Amenomiya Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Takiozuka Kofun|ja|滝大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小田中古墳群|version=100805889|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小田中古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q24876700}} [[Category:Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nakanoto]] [[Category:Nakanoto, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
